speaker: BUSHY
text: Madam, your Majesty is too much sad.

speaker: BUSHY
text: You promised, when you parted with the King,

speaker: BUSHY
text: To lay aside life-harming heaviness

speaker: BUSHY
text: And entertain a cheerful disposition.

speaker: QUEEN
text: To please the King I did; to please myself

speaker: QUEEN
text: I cannot do it. Yet I know no cause

speaker: QUEEN
text: Why I should welcome such a guest as grief,

speaker: QUEEN
text: Save bidding farewell to so sweet a guest

speaker: QUEEN
text: As my sweet Richard. Yet again methinks

speaker: QUEEN
text: Some unborn sorrow ripe in Fortune’s womb

speaker: QUEEN
text: Is coming towards me, and my inward soul

speaker: QUEEN
text: With nothing trembles. At some thing it grieves

speaker: QUEEN
text: More than with parting from my lord the King.

speaker: BUSHY
text: Each substance of a grief hath twenty shadows

speaker: BUSHY
text: Which shows like grief itself but is not so;

speaker: BUSHY
text: For sorrow’s eyes, glazed with blinding tears,

speaker: BUSHY
text: Divides one thing entire to many objects,

speaker: BUSHY
text: Like perspectives, which rightly gazed upon

speaker: BUSHY
text: Show nothing but confusion, eyed awry

speaker: BUSHY
text: Distinguish form. So your sweet Majesty,

speaker: BUSHY
text: Looking awry upon your lord’s departure,

speaker: BUSHY
text: Find shapes of grief more than himself to wail,

speaker: BUSHY
text: Which, looked on as it is, is naught but shadows

speaker: BUSHY
text: Of what it is not. Then, thrice-gracious queen,

speaker: BUSHY
text: More than your lord’s departure weep not. More is not seen,

speaker: BUSHY
text: Or if it be, ’tis with false sorrow’s eye,

speaker: BUSHY
text: Which for things true weeps things imaginary.

speaker: QUEEN
text: It may be so, but yet my inward soul

speaker: QUEEN
text: Persuades me it is otherwise. Howe’er it be,

speaker: QUEEN
text: I cannot but be sad—so heavy sad

speaker: QUEEN
text: As thought, on thinking on no thought I think,

speaker: QUEEN
text: Makes me with heavy nothing faint and shrink.

speaker: BUSHY
text: ’Tis nothing but conceit, my gracious lady.

speaker: QUEEN
text: ’Tis nothing less. Conceit is still derived

speaker: QUEEN
text: From some forefather grief. Mine is not so,

speaker: QUEEN
text: For nothing hath begot my something grief—

speaker: QUEEN
text: Or something hath the nothing that I grieve.

speaker: QUEEN
text: ’Tis in reversion that I do possess,

speaker: QUEEN
text: But what it is that is not yet known what,

speaker: QUEEN
text: I cannot name. ’Tis nameless woe, I wot.

speaker: GREEN
text: God save your Majesty!—And well met, gentlemen.

speaker: GREEN
text: I hope the King is not yet shipped for Ireland.

speaker: QUEEN
text: Why hopest thou so? ’Tis better hope he is,

speaker: QUEEN
text: For his designs crave haste, his haste good hope.

speaker: QUEEN
text: Then wherefore dost thou hope he is not shipped?

speaker: GREEN
text: That he, our hope, might have retired his power

speaker: GREEN
text: And driven into despair an enemy’s hope,

speaker: GREEN
text: Who strongly hath set footing in this land.

speaker: GREEN
text: The banished Bolingbroke repeals himself

speaker: GREEN
text: And with uplifted arms is safe arrived

speaker: GREEN
text: At Ravenspurgh.

speaker: QUEEN
text: Now God in heaven forbid!

speaker: GREEN
text: Ah, madam, ’tis too true. And that is worse,

speaker: GREEN
text: The Lord Northumberland, his son young Harry Percy,

speaker: GREEN
text: The Lords of Ross, Beaumont, and Willoughby,

speaker: GREEN
text: With all their powerful friends, are fled to him.

speaker: BUSHY
text: Why have you not proclaimed Northumberland

speaker: BUSHY
text: And all the rest revolted faction traitors?

speaker: GREEN
text: We have; whereupon the Earl of Worcester

speaker: GREEN
text: Hath broken his staff, resigned his stewardship,

speaker: GREEN
text: And all the Household servants fled with him

speaker: GREEN
text: To Bolingbroke.

speaker: QUEEN
text: So, Green, thou art the midwife to my woe,

speaker: QUEEN
text: And Bolingbroke my sorrow’s dismal heir.

speaker: QUEEN
text: Now hath my soul brought forth her prodigy,

speaker: QUEEN
text: And I, a gasping new-delivered mother,

speaker: QUEEN
text: Have woe to woe, sorrow to sorrow joined.

speaker: BUSHY
text: Despair not, madam.

speaker: QUEEN
text: Who shall hinder me?

speaker: QUEEN
text: I will despair and be at enmity

speaker: QUEEN
text: With cozening hope. He is a flatterer,

speaker: QUEEN
text: A parasite, a keeper-back of death,

speaker: QUEEN
text: Who gently would dissolve the bands of life

speaker: QUEEN
text: Which false hope lingers in extremity.

speaker: GREEN
text: Here comes the Duke of York.

speaker: QUEEN
text: With signs of war about his agèd neck.

speaker: QUEEN
text: O, full of careful business are his looks!—

speaker: QUEEN
text: Uncle, for God’s sake speak comfortable words.

speaker: YORK
text: Should I do so, I should belie my thoughts.

speaker: YORK
text: Comfort’s in heaven, and we are on the Earth,

speaker: YORK
text: Where nothing lives but crosses, cares, and grief.

speaker: YORK
text: Your husband, he is gone to save far off

speaker: YORK
text: Whilst others come to make him lose at home.

speaker: YORK
text: Here am I left to underprop his land,

speaker: YORK
text: Who, weak with age, cannot support myself.

speaker: YORK
text: Now comes the sick hour that his surfeit made;

speaker: YORK
text: Now shall he try his friends that flattered him.

speaker: SERVINGMAN
text: My lord, your son was gone before I came.

speaker: YORK
text: He was? Why, so go all which way it will.

speaker: YORK
text: The nobles they are fled; the commons they are cold

speaker: YORK
text: And will, I fear, revolt on Hereford’s side.

speaker: YORK
text: Sirrah, get thee to Plashy, to my sister Gloucester;

speaker: YORK
text: Bid her send me presently a thousand pound.

speaker: YORK
text: Hold, take my ring.

speaker: SERVINGMAN
text: My lord, I had forgot to tell your Lordship:

speaker: SERVINGMAN
text: Today as I came by I callèd there—

speaker: SERVINGMAN
text: But I shall grieve you to report the rest.

speaker: YORK
text: What is ’t, knave?

speaker: SERVINGMAN
text: An hour before I came, the Duchess died.

speaker: YORK
text: God for His mercy, what a tide of woes

speaker: YORK
text: Comes rushing on this woeful land at once!

speaker: YORK
text: I know not what to do. I would to God,

speaker: YORK
text: So my untruth had not provoked him to it,

speaker: YORK
text: The King had cut off my head with my brother’s!

speaker: YORK
text: What, are there no posts dispatched for Ireland?

speaker: YORK
text: How shall we do for money for these wars?—

speaker: YORK
text: Come, sister—cousin I would say, pray pardon me.—

speaker: YORK
text: Go, fellow, get thee home. Provide some carts

speaker: YORK
text: And bring away the armor that is there.

speaker: YORK
text: Gentlemen, will you go muster men?

speaker: YORK
text: If I know how or which way to order these affairs

speaker: YORK
text: Thus disorderly thrust into my hands,

speaker: YORK
text: Never believe me. Both are my kinsmen.

speaker: YORK
text: T’ one is my sovereign, whom both my oath

speaker: YORK
text: And duty bids defend; t’ other again

speaker: YORK
text: Is my kinsman, whom the King hath wronged,

speaker: YORK
text: Whom conscience and my kindred bids to right.

speaker: YORK
text:
Well, somewhat we must do.
                 
                To
                 
                Queen
                .
               Come, cousin,

speaker: YORK
text: I’ll dispose of you.—Gentlemen, go muster up your men

speaker: YORK
text: And meet me presently at Berkeley.

speaker: YORK
text: I should to Plashy too,

speaker: YORK
text: But time will not permit. All is uneven,

speaker: YORK
text: And everything is left at six and seven.

speaker: BUSHY
text: The wind sits fair for news to go for Ireland,

speaker: BUSHY
text: But none returns. For us to levy power

speaker: BUSHY
text: Proportionable to the enemy

speaker: BUSHY
text: Is all unpossible.

speaker: GREEN
text: Besides, our nearness to the King in love

speaker: GREEN
text: Is near the hate of those love not the King.

speaker: BAGOT
text: And that is the wavering commons, for their love

speaker: BAGOT
text: Lies in their purses, and whoso empties them

speaker: BAGOT
text: By so much fills their hearts with deadly hate.

speaker: BUSHY
text: Wherein the King stands generally condemned.

speaker: BAGOT
text: If judgment lie in them, then so do we,

speaker: BAGOT
text: Because we ever have been near the King.

speaker: GREEN
text: Well, I will for refuge straight to Bristow Castle.

speaker: GREEN
text: The Earl of Wiltshire is already there.

speaker: BUSHY
text: Thither will I with you, for little office

speaker: BUSHY
text: Will the hateful commons perform for us,

speaker: BUSHY
text: Except like curs to tear us all to pieces.—

speaker: BUSHY
text: Will you go along with us?

speaker: BAGOT
text: No, I will to Ireland to his Majesty.

speaker: BAGOT
text: Farewell. If heart’s presages be not vain,

speaker: BAGOT
text: We three here part that ne’er shall meet again.

speaker: BUSHY
text: That’s as York thrives to beat back Bolingbroke.

speaker: GREEN
text: Alas, poor duke, the task he undertakes

speaker: GREEN
text: Is numb’ring sands and drinking oceans dry.

speaker: GREEN
text: Where one on his side fights, thousands will fly.

speaker: GREEN
text: Farewell at once, for once, for all, and ever.

speaker: BUSHY
text: Well, we may meet again.

speaker: BAGOT
text: I fear me, never.
